creator: tijl@ulyssis.org
text:
On Tuesday 21 April 2009 20:37:39 Stanislav Sedov wrote:
> On Tue, 21 Apr 2009 18:20:02 GMT
> Tijl Coosemans <tijl@ulyssis.org> mentioned:
>> I ran into this when updating digikam. Installing .la files is
>> generally accepted nowadays, so I hope Scot's patch can be committed.
>> While rebuilding all lcms dependent ports would probably work it's
>> something that should be avoided if possible. It's way too much work
>> compared to installing a tiny 800 byte .la file.
> 
> That's exactly the reason why .la files should not be installed. In
> fact installing .la on FreeBSD could be considered a bug.

This discussion was held years ago. FreeBSD used to have a patched
libtool that didn't install .la files. It turned out to be too much
trouble for too little gain. Nowadays .la files are installed and
changing that with hacks in the Makefile is just not worth it.
